messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "firmware-security-audit-gate",
 "title": "The firmware/security audit gate precedes every intelligent-PDU listing — and it isn't in the funnel",
 "narrative": "An intelligent PDU is a networked device sitting on the management LAN of critical infrastructure. Before any large CE operator issues a PO, the vendor must clear a security prequalification that a dumb-metal vendor never faces: vulnerability disclosure process, signed-firmware update infrastructure, SBOM, penetration evidence, increasingly IEC 62443-style artifacts as the EU Cyber Resilience Act obligations phase in. This gate is serial with — not parallel to — CE/TÜV product certification, and it is audited against infrastructure the pre-launch venture has to build during the very year YAM assumes it is harvesting. The serviceableFactor's 0.55 haircut names only the classic cert stack; the security gate is a second, slower door in front of the same customers, and it opens per-operator, not per-product.",
 "category": "execution",
 "targetNodes": [
  "obtainableFactor",
  "serviceableFactor"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "Large-operator prequalification for networked power devices requires an auditable firmware update/PSIRT/SBOM capability before AVL listing; the documented EU entry stack (WEEE, CE, TÜV, RoHS, FCC) is already broader than the ledger's derivation names, and cyber requirements sit on top of it",
   "refs": [
    "digest:serviceable-benchmark#c13",
    "digest:serviceable-benchmark#c4",
    "ledger:serviceableFactor"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "A pre-launch entrant builds this infrastructure and passes per-operator audits inside the same 12 months YAM covers; each audit cycle runs one to two quarters, and the primary-buyer choice (large operators) selects exactly the buyers with the heaviest security gates",
   "refs": [
    "profile:Stage",
    "profile:Who is the primary buyer you'll sell through first?"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "The gate serially delays the first PO (compressing the effective selling window inside obtainableFactor) and forecloses security-gated accounts entirely for Year 1 (a reach shortfall inside serviceableFactor) — neither effect appears in the static 0.55 or the 1%",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:obtainableFactor",
    "ledger:serviceableFactor"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "The founder's risk list has a 'certifications' line item, and it looks handled — CE/TÜV are known, scheduled, budgeted. The security audit is invisible from the outside because it is buyer-side process, not published standard: it appears for the first time as a 40-page vendor questionnaire attached to the first serious RFQ, after the model's clock has already started.",
 "expectedObviousness": "low",
 "falsifier": "Two or more large CE operators (or their integrators) issuing rack-PDU RFQs in the venture's first two quarters with no firmware-security/SBOM prequalification requirement, or confirming that product CE marking alone suffices for AVL listing.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.4,
  "rationale": "Security prequalification for networked infrastructure devices is standard practice at hyperscale/large-colo buyers and CRA obligations are phasing in, but mid-market and distributor channels gate far less strictly; probability reflects the venture's explicit large-operator-first choice.",
  "basis": "judgment"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "serviceableFactor",
   "op": "scale",
   "value": 0.8,
   "note": "Security-gated large accounts unreachable within Year 1 despite product certification"
  },
  {
   "nodeId": "obtainableFactor",
   "op": "scale",
   "value": 0.5,
   "note": "First-PO delay of ~2 quarters halves the effective Year-1 selling window"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Vendor-security questionnaire content (SBOM, signed firmware, PSIRT, 62443 mapping) in rack-power prequalification packets",
   "where": "Procurement/supplier portals of Equinix, Digital Realty, NorthC and other CE colo operators; first three RFQs the venture receives",
   "threshold": "≥2 of first 3 RFQs requiring SBOM or firmware-security attestation before quotation",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "CRA horizontal-standard timeline for network-connected industrial products",
   "where": "CEN/CENELEC JTC13 docket and Commission CRA implementing-act publications",
   "threshold": "Harmonized standard citing connected power-distribution devices with obligations landing inside the venture's first 24 months",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Run a mock security audit against two target operators' published supplier requirements pre-launch to size the true listing lead time per account",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "obtainableFactor",
   "note": "Converts an unknown serial gate into a scheduled one; de-risks the Year-1 timing assumption"
  },
  {
   "action": "Ship with a third-party-audited firmware signing and PSIRT process at launch (certifiable artifact, not a roadmap slide), and sequence first sales through channels with lighter security gates (distributors, mid-market) while operator audits run",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": null
  }
 ],
 "lens": "execution-window",
 "riskId": "risk.execution-window.firmware-security-audit-gate"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

This gate is serial with — not parallel to — CE/TÜV product certification, and it is audited against infrastructure the pre-launch venture has to build during the very year YAM assumes it is harvesting. first three RFQs the venture receives","threshold":"≥2 of first 3 RFQs requiring SBOM or firmware-security attestation before quotation","updates":"increases"},{"signal":"CRA horizontal-standard timeline for network-connected industrial products","where":"CEN/CENELEC JTC13 docket and Commission CRA implementing-act publications","threshold":"Harmonized standard citing connected power-distribution devices with obligations landing inside the venture's first 24 months","updates":"increases"}],"mitigations":[{"action":"Run a mock security audit against two target operators' published supplier requirements pre-launch to size the true listing lead time per account","type":"information","voiNodeId":"obtainableFactor","note":"Converts an unknown serial gate into a scheduled one; de-risks the Year-1 timing assumption"},{"action":"Ship with a third-party-audited firmware signing and PSIRT process at launch (certifiable artifact, not a roadmap slide), and sequence first sales through channels with lighter security gates (distributors, mid-market) while operator audits run","type":"strategic","voiNodeId":null,"note":null}]}
